subject: Cloud Computing
title: The SaaS Business Model
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The SaaS Business Model.

hook.text: Selling software used to mean one big sale per customer. SaaS turned it into recurring revenue forever.

buildup.text: Monthly subscriptions create predictable revenue. Investors love SaaS because cash flow is reliable.

discovery.text: The magic metric is 'net revenue retention': do existing customers spend more each year, or less?

twist.text: Great SaaS companies grow even if they stop acquiring new customers. Expansion revenue does the work.

climax.text: SaaS changed how software is built, sold, and valued. It created an entirely new business playbook.

punchline.text: SaaS isn't just technology. It's a business revolution.

quiz.question: Why do investors favor SaaS companies?
options: Recurring subscriptions create predictable, reliable revenue streams
SaaS companies never need to spend money on marketing
SaaS products are always profitable from day one
